atoms.semantic_claim: The old executor-builder trait adds a pool-aware EVM construction hook so specialized EVM builders can access transaction-pool state during block-verification setup while generic builders retain a default fallback.

destinations.evidence: The merge notes explicitly say not to carry forward the old build_evm_with_pool shim. They direct custom execution to the current ExecutorBuilder shape and say async/scheduled txpool behavior should map to PoolBuilder<Node, Evm>, transaction validation, or pool maintenance because the pool now receives the builder-created EVM config directly.

Current component construction builds evm_config with executor_builder.build_evm(context), then calls pool_builder.build_pool(context, evm_config.clone()), then wires network and payload builder with the pool and EVM config. This is the intended v2 ownership direction: EVM config into pool, not pool into EVM construction.

The current PoolBuilder is generic over Evm and build_pool receives evm_config. Concrete maintenance helpers still require TransactionPoolExt and spawn maintain_transaction_pool_future on the provider canonical_state_stream.

When the pool receives canonical-state changes, it updates the normal txpool, processes canonical async commitments into the async lifecycle pool, processes canonical async settlements/deliveries, and cleans up expired async entries. This proves the old pool-aware async lifecycle work moved to pool maintenance rather than EVM construction.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Confirmed after mechanical review: the old ExecutorBuilder::build_evm_with_pool hook is not the v2 replacement path and should not be restored merely for API parity. MERGE_NOTES says not to carry the shim forward; the current executor builder returns EVM config, the pool builder receives that EVM config, and concrete transaction-pool maintenance handles canonical async lifecycle state. The only confirmed non-equivalence from the old pool-aware verifier cluster is synchronous blocking-handler ordering, recorded in file-0194 and file-0197.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Do not add a build_evm_with_pool compile regression. Instead, test the actual desired FSM at the canonical-state/pool-maintenance boundary and the payload/verifier boundaries.
atoms.notes: Reviewed 2026-05-11: build_evm_with_pool absence is intentional architecture, not an unresolved proof item. See files 194/197 for the confirmed remaining blocking-order gap.

summary: Reviewed and downgraded: the old build_evm_with_pool executor-builder hook is intentionally absent per MERGE_NOTES. Current v2 architecture passes EVM config into PoolBuilder and handles pool lifecycle in transaction-pool maintenance/payload code; no unresolved verifier-pool equivalence proof remains in this file.
notes: Reviewed 2026-05-11 against current port code and MERGE_NOTES.
MERGE_NOTES explicitly instructs not to carry forward build_evm_with_pool.
Current ExecutorBuilder exposes build_evm only, while PoolBuilder<Node, Evm> receives evm_config.
Pool async lifecycle state is processed by Pool::on_canonical_state_change from txpool maintenance.
The confirmed missing behavior is synchronous blocking-handler ordering, tracked in files 194 and 197.
